Object.Name: Quality of Service
Object.Description1: This LwM2M Object provides Quality of Service (QoS) configuration and monitoring for the LwM2M Device's network bearers. It allows the LwM2M Server to read and configure QoS parameters such as traffic class, bitrates, packet delay budget, packet error rate, and DSCP markings.

Item.Name: QoS Profile Name
Item.Description: A human-readable name or label identifying this QoS profile instance.

Item.Name: QoS Class Identifier
Item.Description: QoS Class Identifier (QCI) for LTE or 5G QoS Identifier (5QI) for NR bearers. Standardized values are defined in 3GPP TS 23.203 (QCI) and 3GPP TS 23.501 (5QI). A value of 0 indicates that no specific QCI/5QI is assigned.

Item.Name: Traffic Priority
Item.Description: Traffic priority level for this bearer or flow. Higher values indicate higher priority. 0: Best effort (lowest priority) 1..6: Increasing priority levels 7: Highest priority This value is used to differentiate traffic flows when scheduling resources.

Item.Name: Maximum Bitrate Uplink
Item.Description: Maximum bitrate (MBR) in the uplink direction for this bearer or flow, expressed in bits per second. A value of 0 indicates no restriction is applied.

Item.Name: Maximum Bitrate Downlink
Item.Description: Maximum bitrate (MBR) in the downlink direction for this bearer or flow, expressed in bits per second. A value of 0 indicates no restriction is applied.

Item.Name: Guaranteed Bitrate Uplink
Item.Description: Guaranteed bitrate (GBR) in the uplink direction for this bearer or flow, expressed in bits per second. Applicable only for GBR bearers. A value of 0 indicates a non-GBR bearer.

Item.Name: Guaranteed Bitrate Downlink
Item.Description: Guaranteed bitrate (GBR) in the downlink direction for this bearer or flow, expressed in bits per second. Applicable only for GBR bearers. A value of 0 indicates a non-GBR bearer.

Item.Name: Packet Delay Budget
Item.Description: The Packet Delay Budget (PDB) defines an upper bound for the time that a packet may be delayed between the UE and the PDCP layer of the eNB/gNB, expressed in milliseconds. A value of 0 indicates no PDB is configured.

Item.Name: Packet Error Rate
Item.Description: The Packet Error Rate (PER) defines an upper bound for the rate of packets that have been processed by the sender of a link layer protocol but not received by the receiver of that protocol, expressed as a value between 0 and 1 (e.g. 0.001 for 10^-3). A value of 0 indicates no PER requirement is configured.

Item.Name: DSCP Marking
Item.Description: Differentiated Services Code Point (DSCP) value to be applied to IP packets belonging to this QoS flow, as defined in RFC 2474. The 6-bit DSCP value ranges from 0 to 63. Common values include: 0 (000000): Best Effort (BE) 10 (001010): AF11 46 (101110): Expedited Forwarding (EF) A value of 0 indicates Best Effort forwarding.

Item.Name: Allocation and Retention Priority
Item.Description: Allocation and Retention Priority (ARP) value as defined in 3GPP TS 23.203. The ARP is used to decide whether a bearer establishment or modification request can be accepted, and also for deciding which bearers to discard in case of resource limitations. Lower values indicate higher priority (1 = highest, 15 = lowest).

Item.Name: Preemption Capability
Item.Description: Indicates whether a service data flow may get resources that were already assigned to another service data flow with a lower ARP priority level. True = the bearer may preempt resources from a lower-priority bearer. False = the bearer shall not preempt resources from lower-priority bearers.

Item.Name: Preemption Vulnerability
Item.Description: Indicates whether a service data flow may lose the resources assigned to it in order to admit a service data flow with a higher ARP priority level. True = resources assigned to this bearer may be preempted by a higher-priority bearer. False = resources shall not be preempted.

Item.Name: QoS Monitoring Enabled
Item.Description: Indicates whether QoS monitoring is currently active for this object instance. When set to True, the device should collect and report QoS metrics. When set to False, monitoring is disabled.

Item.Name: Measured Uplink Throughput
Item.Description: The current measured uplink throughput for this QoS flow, expressed in bits per second. This is a read-only, observed value reported by the device.

Item.Name: Measured Downlink Throughput
Item.Description: The current measured downlink throughput for this QoS flow, expressed in bits per second. This is a read-only, observed value reported by the device.

Item.Name: Measured Round-Trip Delay
Item.Description: The current measured round-trip delay (RTD) for this QoS flow, expressed in milliseconds. This is a read-only, observed value reported by the device.

Object.Description2: Multiple instances of this object may be used to represent different QoS profiles or bearers active on the device simultaneously.
